I’ll submit one big reimbursement request once the accounting mess is cleaned up.

"Since Melissa stepped down over the car wash money issue, I’ve been putting the winter league snacks and new cones/pinnies on my card. I’ll file for Booster reimbursement once we get a new treasurer in place."

artifacts.content:
Hi Mom,

Finally sitting down for a minute, so I wanted to catch you up on a few things before I forget.

First, I’m turning in that big reimbursement request for the girls’ soccer stuff this weekend. It covers all the pre-season training gear and the healthy post-game meals I’ve been buying between late November 2025 and early January 2026 – the pinnies, cones, keeper gloves, resistance bands, plus the wraps/fruit/water for the 12/2, 12/16, and 1/6 winter league games at Mountlake Terrace High School. I put everything on my personal Visa and labeled each receipt by date and opponent (Shorewood, Meadowdale, Everett) so it’s not a total mess.

Honestly, I had to do it this way because Jason missed two consecutive child-support payments in November and December, and I couldn’t float those soccer expenses any longer without risking late fees. I did NOT want to put any of it on my Cascade Insight company card and then have to explain why I was late paying it off, so the personal Visa took the hit. I’m hoping the Booster check comes through quickly so I can get that balance back down before interest kicks in.

Kids are otherwise fine. Emily’s still buried in soccer and homework, and Noah is obsessed with his robotics club at Brier Terrace. I’ll bring them by Shoreline next Sunday if that still works – I can grab a rotisserie chicken on the way so you don’t have to stand too long on those knees.

Love you,
Sarah

metadata.subject: Quick update + soccer stuff

artifacts.content:
Notes – Soccer / Money / Family (updated 2026-01-09)

1. Booster reimbursement
- Submit full packet for MTHS Girls Varsity Soccer this weekend.
- Covers late November 2025 – early January 2026 purchases.
- Training gear: 24 reversible numbered pinnies, 40 disc cones, 12 tall cones, 2 pairs GK gloves, 5-pack resistance bands.
- Food for home winter league games on 12/02, 12/16, 01/06 – turkey/veggie wraps, fruit, granola bars, string cheese, bottled water, GF/DF options.
- Total: $486.73.
- Check should be made payable to “Sarah L. Mitchell” and mailed to: 23014 52nd Ave W, Apt 3B, Mountlake Terrace, WA 98043.
- Coach Daniel Ruiz confirmed via email on 2026-01-08 that everything fits the Girls Soccer Booster budget.

2. Credit card / Jason / Emily
- Remember: all soccer expenses went on same personal Visa as Emily’s weekly therapy sessions at Sound Pathways Counseling in Lynnwood (since her panic attack after the 2025-10-28 Shorewood away game).
- Jason missed November + December child-support payments, so I’m extra tight until reimbursement hits.
- Priority: pay down Visa before next cycle of therapy charges.

3. Work / Mom
- December 2025: reduced hours at Cascade Insight a few weeks to take Mom (Linda) to Swedish Medical Center – Edmonds for oncology follow-ups. Need to watch PTO balance.
- Don’t overbook mornings on days after late winter league games; blood pressure was borderline at last physical.

4. To-do
- Scan any missing receipts.
- Update budget spreadsheet.
- Walk Terrace Creek Park trail at least twice this week.

Hey Alex,

Catching you between your Portland life and my endless soccer shuttle runs.

December was a blur. I cut my hours at Cascade Insight for a few weeks so I could take Mom to her oncology follow-up appointments at Swedish Medical Center – Edmonds. Sitting in that waiting room with her while they checked labs and scans was… a lot. She puts on a brave face, but walking from the car into the clinic really wiped her out a couple of times. I’m glad I was there, but the reduced work hours definitely hit my paycheck.

At the same time, I somehow became the de facto logistics person for MTHS Girls Varsity Soccer winter league. I’ve been fronting the pre-season training gear (pinnies, cones, resistance bands, keeper gloves) and all the post-game food for the home games. I’m putting it all on my personal Visa and then getting reimbursed by the Girls Soccer Boosters.

The timing was awful: smaller December paycheck because of Mom’s appointments, plus Jason missing two child-support payments in a row. Between that and Emily’s weekly therapy at Sound Pathways Counseling in Lynnwood after her panic attack following the 10/28 Shorewood away game, the Visa balance looks scary. I’m submitting the big reimbursement request this week so it hopefully hits before the next round of therapy charges.

Otherwise, kids are okay. Emily’s talking more about college (help) and Noah is still building robots out of anything not nailed down. How are things with you and work? Any chance you’re up here in February? If so, we should coordinate around one of Em’s games at Mountlake Terrace High School so you can actually see her play.

Love,
Sarah

metadata.subject: Mom, money, and soccer circus

artifacts.content:
Hey Jason,

Logistics first:
- You’ve got Noah this coming Thursday–Sunday, right? His robotics club at Brier Terrace meets Thu 3–4:30 p.m., so if you can grab him from there instead of the apartment, that would be easier.
- Emily’s winter league game at MTHS is Friday at 7:15 p.m. (home). I’ll be there early setting up food.

Money stuff (sorry, but we have to talk about it):
I ended up putting the girls’ new training gear and the post-game food for the 12/2, 12/16, and 1/6 games on the same personal Visa we’re using for Emily’s weekly therapy sessions at Sound Pathways Counseling in Lynnwood. That’s the card that’s been getting hit every week since her panic attack after the 10/28 Shorewood away game.

I’m filing a reimbursement with the MTHS Girls Soccer Boosters for the pinnies, cones, keeper gloves, resistance bands, and all the post-game meals. Coach Ruiz already confirmed everything lines up with the budget. But until that check comes through, the balance is pretty high, especially with your missed November and December child-support payments.

I’m not trying to start a fight; I just need you to understand why I’m stressed about the timing. If you can catch up on at least one of those payments this month, it would help a lot. I really don’t want to cut back on Emily’s therapy sessions just to keep up with interest on the card.

Let’s keep the kids out of this and just sort it out between us.

Sarah

metadata.subject: Kids’ schedule + card situation

artifacts.content:
Journal – 2025-11-22 (late night, kitchen table)

Paycheck yesterday was short by $350 because of that stupid Snohomish County District Court judgment over the parking ticket I swear I paid two years ago. HR at Cascade Insight said the school district processed the garnishment on 11/15 and there was nothing they could do.

I keep thinking about how this looks from the outside: single mom, wage garnishment, juggling rent on the Mountlake Terrace apartment and kids’ activities. I don’t want anyone at MTHS Girls Soccer thinking I’m backing out of helping just because money is tight.

On 11/20 I emailed Coach Daniel Ruiz and told him I’d go ahead and buy the extra cones, numbered pinnies, keeper gloves, and resistance bands on my personal card and then get reimbursed by the Girls Soccer Boosters later. I also volunteered to handle the post-game food for the winter league home dates – healthier stuff like wraps and fruit instead of just doughnuts. Part of me worries I’m overcompensating because I’m embarrassed about the garnishment, but I really do want the girls to have what they need.

Plan:
- Track every soccer purchase (late November through December 2025) in the budget spreadsheet.
- Keep receipts organized by date and opponent.
- Once I’ve got a solid total, submit for reimbursement so I can wipe that chunk off the Visa before interest hits.

For now: make tea, breathe, and remember that one bad paycheck doesn’t define me.

Hi Megan,

I owe you a coffee and a giant rant, but this email will have to do for now.

You know how I was already feeling stretched with the Cascade Insight accounts and all of Emily’s soccer stuff? Well, Snohomish County garnished $350 from my 11/15 paycheck over that ancient parking ticket judgment. So when I told you I felt like my paycheck “shrunk in the wash,” I wasn’t kidding.

To make things extra fun, I agreed with Coach Ruiz in an email on 11/20 that I’d front the winter league expenses for MTHS Girls Varsity Soccer – cones, reversible numbered pinnies, keeper gloves, resistance bands, plus post-game food for the home games – and then get reimbursed by the Girls Soccer Boosters. I didn’t want it to look like I was backing out of helping the team right when we’re trying to build momentum for spring.

Meanwhile, at home, Emily’s still going to weekly therapy at Sound Pathways Counseling in Lynnwood after that panic attack she had following the 10/28 away game at Shorewood. Those sessions are on the same Visa I’m using for the soccer stuff. Add in Jason spacing on two months of child support and December’s reduced hours when I took Mom to her oncology follow-ups at Swedish Edmonds, and you can imagine the spreadsheet chaos.

I keep telling myself this is temporary: reimbursement check comes in, child support catches up, my hours stabilize, and the card balance goes back to something that doesn’t make my eye twitch.

Anyway, next time we’re both in the office downtown, I’m claiming you for a lunch walk.

– Sarah

metadata.subject: Why my budget spreadsheet is screaming

artifacts.content:
Booster / Soccer Admin Brain Dump – 2025-12-07

1. Treasurer chaos
Early December: Melissa Harding resigned as Girls Soccer treasurer after that argument with the Mountlake Terrace High School main office over how she handled the cash from the fall 2025 car wash fundraiser. Main office wanted every dollar deposited same-day; she’d been holding some to reimburse parents in cash. Ugly emails, then she just quit.

Result: no one to cut checks or track winter league expenses, so I basically stepped in by default.

2. What I’m covering
Since Melissa stepped down, I’ve been buying:
- Pre-season training gear: new reversible numbered pinnies, extra cones, resistance bands, GK gloves.
- Post-game food for winter league home games (starting with 12/02 vs Shorewood): wraps, fruit, granola bars, water, plus GF/DF items.

All going on my personal Visa for now, on top of Emily’s weekly therapy at Sound Pathways Counseling in Lynnwood (post-10/28 Shorewood panic attack) and regular life stuff.

3. Reimbursement plan
- Work with Coach Ruiz and whoever becomes the new treasurer to reconcile everything.
- Submit one consolidated reimbursement request covering late November 2025 through early January 2026.
- Make sure check is issued to “Sarah L. Mitchell” and mailed to the apartment so I can knock down the card balance.

4. To remember
- Don’t let Booster drama bleed into how I talk about soccer with Emily.
- Keep receipts labeled by date/opponent.
- Breathe. This is temporary.
